anti_claim: This standard is a public-safe first-entry walkthrough schema only; it does not authorize release, hosting, publication, recipient work, provider calls, private-data equivalence, or whole-system correctness.

anti_claim_contract.rule: public reveal material must state what the walkthrough does not authorize

anti_claims: This public standard does not prove runtime completeness or publication readiness.
This public standard does not claim private macro-system equivalence.

omission_receipt.reason: public reveal carries commands, refs, and anti-claims only

omission_receipt_contract.rule: walkthrough receipts must list omitted authority surfaces

receipt_expectations: receipt must cite the ten-minute steps, command refs, evidence refs, negative-case coverage, private-state scan, and anti-claim

source_coupling_contract.rule: walkthrough refs must stay public-root relative or explicit localhost URLs

standard_payload.anti_purpose: Marketing-only reveal copy without runnable command or evidence refs.
failure_modes: first entry requires private Atlas knowledge
walkthrough hides evidence behind raw JSON first
standard_payload.ontology: A short public entry path that ties product claim, commands, substrate refs, evidence refs, and authority ceiling.
standard_payload.teleology: Make Microcosm legible to a cold technical reader in under ten minutes.

validation_rules: require at least five walkthrough steps within a ten-minute budget
require commands and evidence refs for each reveal move
reject release, hosting, publication, provider, private-equivalence, and whole-system-correctness overclaims
require private-state scan and redacted receipts
